checks.describe: host Ollama serves the eval judge model qwen3:0.6b (Step 0). Self-seeds by pulling it if absent
checks.run: ollama list 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'qwen3:0.6b' || ollama pull qwen3:0.6b >/dev/null 2>&1; if ollama list 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'qwen3:0.6b'; then echo judge-ready; else echo judge-missing; fi

checks.describe: the app reads its system prompt from a file (SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE) with an inline fallback — the prompt-as-config change (Step 2)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/app/main.py; if grep -q 'SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE' "$F" && grep -q 'load_system_prompt' "$F" && grep -q 'DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT' "$F"; then echo prompt-config; else echo prompt-inline; fi

checks.describe: the versioned system prompts exist — system.txt (v1) and system-v2.txt (the A/B variant) (Step 2, Step 6)
checks.run: if [ -s labs/opsmate/prompts/system.txt ] && [ -s labs/opsmate/prompts/system-v2.txt ]; then echo prompts-present; else echo prompts-missing; fi

checks.describe: the compose app service mounts ./prompts read-only and points SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE at it (Step 2)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/compose.yaml; if grep -q './prompts:/prompts:ro' "$F" && grep -q 'SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE' "$F"; then echo mount-ok; else echo mount-missing; fi

checks.describe: the stack is up and the app loaded its prompt from the mounted file, reported by /prompt (Step 1, Step 2). Self-seeds the stack if the app is not answering
checks.run: curl -fsS --max-time 3 http://localhost:8001/health >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate up >/dev/null 2>&1; for i in $(seq 1 30); do curl -fsS --max-time 3 http://localhost:8001/health >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done; curl -s --max-time 10 http://localhost:8001/prompt | jq -r '.source' | grep -c 'prompts/system' | xargs -I{} sh -c 'if [ {} -ge 1 ]; then echo prompt-loaded; else echo prompt-default; fi'

checks.describe: the golden set exists with the three layers (retrieval, generation, honesty) and every question carries a source (Step 3)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/evals/golden.yaml; R=$(grep -c 'layer: retrieval' "$F"); G=$(grep -c 'layer: generation' "$F"); H=$(grep -c 'layer: honesty' "$F"); if [ "$R" -ge 8 ] && [ "$G" -ge 8 ] && [ "$H" -ge 2 ]; then echo golden-shape-ok; else echo golden-shape-bad; fi

checks.describe: the Chroma index the golden set evaluates against is built and non-empty (Step 1). Self-seeds the stack and ingests if the index is empty
checks.run: curl -fsS --max-time 3 http://localhost:8001/health >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate up >/dev/null 2>&1; [ "$(curl -s http://localhost:8001/health | jq -r '.chunks // 0')" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null || curl -s --max-time 180 -X POST http://localhost:8001/ingest >/dev/null 2>&1; C=$(curl -s --max-time 10 http://localhost:8001/health | jq -r '.chunks // 0'); if [ "$C" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then echo index-built; else echo index-empty; fi

checks.describe: the deterministic retrieval-layer runner reads golden.yaml, hits /retrieve, and writes a machine-readable summary with a majority of checks passing (Step 4). Self-seeds stack + index
checks.run: curl -fsS --max-time 3 http://localhost:8001/health >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate up >/dev/null 2>&1; [ "$(curl -s http://localhost:8001/health | jq -r '.chunks // 0')" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null || curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8001/ingest >/dev/null 2>&1; APP_URL=http://localhost:8001 uv run --quiet labs/m5/retrieval_check.py >/dev/null 2>&1; P=$(jq -r '.passed // 0' labs/opsmate/evals/retrieval-latest.json 2>/dev/null); T=$(jq -r '.total // 0' labs/opsmate/evals/retrieval-latest.json 2>/dev/null); if [ "$T" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null && [ "$P" -ge "$(( (T + 1) / 2 ))" ] 2>/dev/null; then echo retrieval-ok; else echo retrieval-bad; fi

checks.describe: the promptfoo generation tests are generated from the golden set (Step 5) and contain the graded questions
checks.run: uv run --quiet labs/m5/make_promptfoo_tests.py >/dev/null 2>&1; F=labs/opsmate/evals/generation-tests.yaml; if [ -s "$F" ] && grep -q 'icontains-any' "$F" && grep -q 'llm-rubric' "$F"; then echo tests-generated; else echo tests-missing; fi

checks.describe: promptfoo runs the generation layer against /ask and writes a scored results JSON with per-test pass/fail (Step 5). Self-seeds stack + index + tests; graded by the local judge, so allow a long timeout
checks.run: curl -fsS --max-time 3 http://localhost:8001/health >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate up >/dev/null 2>&1; [ "$(curl -s http://localhost:8001/health | jq -r '.chunks // 0')" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null || curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8001/ingest >/dev/null 2>&1; uv run --quiet labs/m5/make_promptfoo_tests.py >/dev/null 2>&1; APP_URL=http://localhost:8001 npx -y promptfoo@latest eval -c labs/opsmate/evals/promptfooconfig.yaml -o labs/opsmate/evals/generation-latest.json --max-concurrency 2 >/dev/null 2>&1; N=$(jq -r '[.results.results[] | select(.success == true or .success == false)] | length' labs/opsmate/evals/generation-latest.json 2>/dev/null); if [ "$N" -ge 1 ] 2>/dev/null; then echo generation-scored; else echo generation-unscored; fi

checks.describe: the versioned baseline template exists with both arms (no-RAG vs RAG) so the lab can record real numbers into it (Step 7)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/evals/baseline.md; if [ -s "$F" ] && grep -qi 'no-RAG' "$F" && grep -qi 'yardstick' "$F"; then echo baseline-present; else echo baseline-missing; fi
